item.title: Docsumo
item.description: Building backend systems for document extraction, table parsing, and fraud analytics in banking workflows.
item.content:
## Junior Backend Engineer - [Docsumo](https://support.docsumo.com/docs/usa-bank-statements) **June 2024 - Present**
I work on backend systems that keep document extraction pipelines reliable. The day-to-day is a mix of careful API changes and deeper experiments around parsing tables accurately. I care a lot about correctness here, because downstream analytics depend on clean, consistent outputs.
- Maintain and improve APIs that power document extraction workflows. - Run focused experiments to improve table extraction quality.
## Python Intern - [Docsumo](https://support.docsumo.com/docs/bank-statement-analytics) **November 2023 - June 2024**
This role taught me how to think about fraud detection and analytics as real product features rather than one-off scripts. I worked closely with the R&D team to iterate quickly while keeping the output explainable for banking use cases.
- Developed fraud detection logic for bank statement analysis. - Built analytics features used by banking clients. - Partnered with R&D to strengthen security-focused workflows.

item.title: Solar Labs
item.description: Backend internship building REST APIs and improving reliability for solar design platform
item.content:
## Backend Developer Intern - [The Solar Labs](https://www.arka360.com/) **July 2022 - April 2023**
My focus here was shipping practical backend improvements that kept the product stable as features grew. I built APIs, worked through performance bottlenecks, and learned how CI/CD discipline keeps small teams moving fast without breaking production.
- Built REST APIs in Python with Django/DRF for product features. - Improved backend services and database performance hotspots. - Used Azure Pipelines to keep deployments consistent and repeatable.

item.title: GeeksForGeeks
item.description: Technical writing focused on clear, practical programming guides.
item.content:
## Technical Content Writer - [GeeksForGeeks](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/) **June 2021 - September 2021**
Writing here sharpened my ability to explain systems clearly. I focused on tutorials that were grounded in runnable steps, with enough context for beginners and enough rigor for readers to trust the details.
- Authored 60+ articles on shell scripting, Linux, and web development. - Contributed to tutorials that emphasize clarity, examples, and reproducibility.
Link to all the articles published by me: [meetgor](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/profile/meetgor?tab=articles)
